html:
“When held, it increases speed by 25% and can teleport the user to visible locations at the cost of some sanity.”

-

The Lazy Explorer is a craftable Item found in the Ancient Pseudoscience Filter. It requires 2 Nightmare Fuel, 1 Walking Cane, 2 Orange Gems, and for player to be near an Ancient Pseudoscience Station to craft. It also can randomly drop from an Ancient Pseudoscience Station when the latter is damaged with a Hammer, Gunpowder etc., and has a 25% chance to be found inside a Large Ornate Chest. Like all items from the Ancient Tab, Lazy Explorers cannot be prototyped and Characters always need to be near an Ancient Pseudoscience Station to craft it again.

When equipped in the hand slot, a prompt to "Telepoof" appears where the cursor is on the field, and right-clicking will almost immediately teleport the Character to that spot. The Lazy Explorer also increases movement speed by 25% while equipped, same as the Walking Cane. Telepoofing will not work on spots where the cursor is unavailable, such as the Ocean or in the dark. With each use, Characters will lose 15 and The Lazy Explorer will lose 5% of its Durability.

Orange Gems are renewable via killing Fuelweaver and Dragonfly and other means, making the Lazy Explorer a renewable item.

Skins

Main article: Belongings

  • The Woven - Elegant Rod of Discord skin was introduced in the An Eye for An Eye update in 2021, as a crossover collaboration with Re-Logic's Terraria. Its rarity was Inspired - Elegant initially, but received change in August 19, 2022.
Woven - Elegant
An Eye for an Eye Collection
Rod of Discord
Imbue your skips with chaotic charm.

Compatible skins for the Walking Cane:

Candy Explorer
See ingame

(associated with: )
Corrupted Staff
See ingame

(associated with: )
Foolish Explorer

(associated with: )
Fanciful Explorer
See ingame

(associated with: )
The Ancient Explorer
See ingame

(associated with: )
Romantic Jaunter

(associated with: )


Tips

  • It is a good idea to equip a Construction Amulet when crafting The Lazy Explorer to save on the rare Orange Gems.
  • It is advised not to have the staff equipped while planting, or performing any other activity that involves right-clicking. The default right-click option is to cast the telepoof spell, resulting in wasted durability if the mouse is clicked in an unacceptable spot.
  • One can stack the speed bonus with the Magiluminescence to have a total increase of 45% movement speed while both items are worn, which means that while on a Road, Characters will have a terrific 75% speed boost.
    • This can be faster with WX-78's "SYSTEM OVERLOAD" or Wormwood's Bloom ability.
    • The speed multiplier is product of all multiplier of equipped items and roads and characters.
  • When teleporting across gaps, any followers the player had at the moment (including Chester) will teleport off-screen near your location.

Trivia

  • Before it was added to the game with The Stuff of Nightmares update, the Lazy Explorer's casting was the same as the other Ancient Staffs.
  • Wendy's quote ("Each time I use it I die and am reborn.") is related to the theory about teleportation that, if a human being were to teleport, their body's atoms would be deconstructed and replaced elsewhere, essentially killing and then recreating the person in question.
  • The Lazy Explorer's damage used to be 10 and was then changed to match the Walking Cane at 17 damage.

Bugs

  • Teleporting across gaps may cause the player to land off the map.
  • Occasionally if the player teleports too quickly, the sound effect of swinging will be played, however the rod will not swing, and the player will also not teleport. This can be fixed by walking a short distance.

Assets

  • Romantic Jaunter Equipped
  • Romantic Jaunter Dropped
  • Romantic Jaunter Icon

Gallery

  • Wolfgang beside and holding The Lazy Explorer.
  • Telepoofing.
  • The Lazy Explorer before it was officially released.
  • A bugged Lazy Explorer at 105% durability.

The Lazy Explorer is a craftable Item found in the Ancient Pseudoscience Filter. It requires 2 Nightmare Fuel, 1 Walking Cane, 2 Orange Gems, and for player to be near an Ancient Pseudoscience Station to craft. It also can randomly drop from an Ancient Pseudoscience Station when the latter is damaged with a Hammer, Gunpowder etc., and has a 25% chance to be found inside a Large Ornate Chest. Like all items from the Ancient Tab, Lazy Explorers cannot be prototyped and Characters always need to be near an Ancient Pseudoscience Station to craft it again.
When equipped in the hand slot, a prompt to "Telepoof" appears where the cursor is on the field, and right-clicking will almost immediately teleport the Character to that spot. The Lazy Explorer also increases movement speed by 25% while equipped, same as the Walking Cane. Telepoofing will not work on spots where the cursor is unavailable, such as the Ocean or in the dark. With each use, Characters will lose 15 and The Lazy Explorer will lose 5% of its Durability.
Orange Gems are renewable via killing Fuelweaver and Dragonfly and other means, making the Lazy Explorer a renewable item.

Tips[edit | edit source]
It is a good idea to equip a Construction Amulet when crafting The Lazy Explorer to save on the rare Orange Gems.
It is advised not to have the staff equipped while planting, or performing any other activity that involves right-clicking. The default right-click option is to cast the telepoof spell, resulting in wasted durability if the mouse is clicked in an unacceptable spot.
One can stack the speed bonus with the Magiluminescence to have a total increase of 45% movement speed while both items are worn, which means that while on a Road, Characters will have a terrific 75% speed boost.
This can be faster with WX-78's "SYSTEM OVERLOAD" or Wormwood's Bloom ability.
The speed multiplier is product of all multiplier of equipped items and roads and characters.
When teleporting across gaps, any followers the player had at the moment (including Chester) will teleport off-screen near your location.
Trivia[edit | edit source]
Before it was added to the game with The Stuff of Nightmares update, the Lazy Explorer's casting was the same as the other Ancient Staffs.
Wendy's quote ("Each time I use it I die and am reborn.") is related to the theory about teleportation that, if a human being were to teleport, their body's atoms would be deconstructed and replaced elsewhere, essentially killing and then recreating the person in question.
The Lazy Explorer's damage used to be 10 and was then changed to match the Walking Cane at 17 damage.
Bugs[edit | edit source]
Teleporting across gaps may cause the player to land off the map.
Occasionally if the player teleports too quickly, the sound effect of swinging will be played, however the rod will not swing, and the player will also not teleport. This can be fixed by walking a short distance.

{{Navbutton/Separation}} {{Object Infobox |icon = The Lazy Explorer.png |equipslot=hands |image = The Lazy Explorer Dropped.png |scrapbook_description =When held, it increases speed by 25% and can teleport the user to visible locations at the cost of some sanity. |tier = 6 |filter = {{Pic32|Ancient Pseudoscience Filter}} |ingredient1 = Nightmare Fuel |multiplier1 = 2 |ingredient2 = Walking Cane |multiplier2 = 1 |ingredient3 = Orange Gem |multiplier3 = 2 |perk = Short range teleportation, 25% faster movement speed |stack = Does not stack |objectSanity= -15 per use |durability = 20 Uses |spawnCode = "orangestaff" |droppedBy = [[File:Ornate Chest.png|32px|link=Ornate Chest#Large Ornate Chest]], [[File:Icon Ancient.png|24px|Ancient Pseudoscience Station]] |description = For those who don't like walking. |damage=17 |haunt = 25% chance to teleport the nearest non-player-ghost a short distance }} {{Quotes |wilson = This beats walking. |willow = It gives me a headache. |wolfgang = Better to walk I think. |wendy = With each use the wielder dies and is again reborn. |wx78 = TEMPORAL DISPLACEMENT TECHNOLOGY |wickerbottom = Handy over small distances. |woodie = This will help me get around quickly. |maxwell = It's a focusing tool. |wigfrid = A staff öf magical mövement. |webber = Teleportation gives us a chance to rest all our feet. |winona = For people who fear good, honest work. |warly = When I hold it it makes the world feel... fast. |wortox = A touch of magic not my own. |wormwood = Poof stick |wurt = Orangey rock stick! |walter = I bet I could race you now, Woby! |wanda = Really? We're just putting the gem on a stick and calling it a day? |choose = wendy }} {{Scrapbook Info|When held, it increases speed by 25% and can teleport the user to visible locations at the cost of some sanity.}} '''The Lazy Explorer''' is a [[:Category:Craftable Items|craftable]] [[Item]] found in the [[Ancient Pseudoscience Filter]]. It requires 2 [[Nightmare Fuel]], 1 [[Walking Cane]], 2 [[Orange Gem]]s, and for player to be near an [[Ancient Pseudoscience Station]] to craft. It also can randomly drop from an Ancient Pseudoscience Station when the latter is damaged with a [[Hammer]], [[Gunpowder]] etc., and has a 25% chance to be found inside a [[Ornate Chest|Large Ornate Chest]]. Like all items from the Ancient Tab, Lazy Explorers cannot be prototyped and [[Characters]] always need to be near an Ancient Pseudoscience Station to craft it again. When equipped in the hand slot, a prompt to "Telepoof" appears where the cursor is on the field, and right-clicking will almost immediately teleport the Character to that spot. The Lazy Explorer also increases movement [[Player Speed|speed]] by 25% while equipped, same as the Walking Cane. Telepoofing will not work on spots where the cursor is unavailable, such as the [[Ocean]] or in the dark. With each use, Characters will lose 15 {{sanity}} and The Lazy Explorer will lose 5% of its [[Durability]]. Orange Gems are renewable via killing [[Reanimated Skeleton|Fuelweaver]] and [[Dragonfly]] and other means, making the Lazy Explorer a renewable item. == [[File:Skins Icon.png|32px|link=]] Skins == {{Main|Belongings#Staffs Skins|Belongings}}
* The '''Woven - Elegant Rod of Discord''' skin was introduced in the ''[[An Eye for An Eye]]'' update in 2021, as a crossover collaboration with Re-Logic's ''Terraria''. Its rarity was '''Inspired - Elegant''' initially, but received change in August 19, 2022.
{{SkinInfo |image = Rod of Discord Icon.png |rarity = Woven - Elegant |collection = An Eye for an Eye Collection |name = Rod of Discord |description = Imbue your skips with chaotic charm. }}
Compatible skins for the [[Walking Cane]]:
{{SkinInfo |image = Candy Cane - The Lazy Explorer Icon.png |name = Candy Explorer |associated = Candy Cane Icon.png |ingame = Candy Explorer Wilson.png }} {{SkinInfo |image = Corrupted Cane - The Lazy Explorer Icon.png |name = Corrupted Staff |associated = Corrupted Cane Icon.png |ingame = Corrupted Staff Wilson.png }} {{SkinInfo |image = Fool's Marotte - The Lazy Explorer Icon.png |name = Foolish Explorer |associated = Fool's Marotte Icon.png }} {{SkinInfo |image = Goatshead Cane - The Lazy Explorer Icon.png |name = Fanciful Explorer |associated = Goatshead Cane Icon.png |ingame = Fanciful Explorer Wilson.png }} {{SkinInfo |image = Ancient Cane - The Lazy Explorer Icon.png |name = The Ancient Explorer |associated = Ancient Cane Icon.png |ingame = The Ancient Explorer Wilson.png }} {{SkinInfo |image = Rosy Red Cane - The Lazy Explorer Icon.png |name = Romantic Jaunter |associated = Rosy Red Cane Icon.png }}
== {{pic32|Prototype}} Tips == * It is a good idea to equip a [[Construction Amulet]] when crafting The Lazy Explorer to save on the rare Orange Gems. * It is advised not to have the staff equipped while planting, or performing any other activity that involves right-clicking. The default right-click option is to cast the telepoof spell, resulting in wasted durability if the mouse is clicked in an unacceptable spot. * One can stack the speed bonus with the [[Magiluminescence]] to have a total increase of 45% movement speed while both items are worn, which means that while on a [[Road]], Characters will have a terrific 75% speed boost. ** This can be faster with [[WX-78]]'s "SYSTEM OVERLOAD" or [[Wormwood]]'s Bloom ability. ** The speed multiplier is product of all multiplier of equipped items and roads and characters. * When teleporting across gaps, any followers the player had at the moment (including [[Chester]]) will teleport off-screen near your location. == {{Pic32|Placeholder}} Trivia == * Before it was added to the game with ''[[Don't Starve/Version History#The Stuff of Nightmares|The Stuff of Nightmares]]'' update, the Lazy Explorer's casting was the same as the other Ancient [[:Category:Staffs|Staffs]]. * Wendy's quote ("Each time I use it I die and am reborn.") is related to the theory about teleportation that, if a human being were to teleport, their body's atoms would be deconstructed and replaced elsewhere, essentially killing and then recreating the person in question. * The Lazy Explorer's damage used to be 10 and was then changed to match the Walking Cane at 17 damage. == {{Pic32|Mosquito}} Bugs == * Teleporting across gaps may cause the player to land off the map. * Occasionally if the player teleports too quickly, the sound effect of swinging will be played, however the rod will not swing, and the player will also not teleport.
